trade.summary: The United States is one of the UAE's largest trading partners. The US exports aircraft, vehicles, machinery, weapons, and agricultural products to the UAE. The UAE exports crude oil and re-exports goods. Dubai is the largest re-export hub for US goods entering the Middle East and Africa. The UAE is the US's largest Arab export market.
disputes: US concerns over UAE-China ties (Huawei, military facility)
US arms sales conditions (F-35 linked to Huawei cancellation)

diplomatic.summary: The UAE-US relationship is one of America's most important Arab partnerships, anchored by the US military presence and decades of security cooperation. Relations have been complicated by UAE's refusal to fully align with US positions on China (Huawei 5G, military facility at Khalifa Port) and Russia (Ukraine sanctions). The UAE sees itself as a hedge-power and resists full Western alignment. Nonetheless, the relationship remains fundamentally strong.
recent_incidents: UAE abstained on UN Russia-Ukraine resolutions
2021 Huawei 5G contract cancelled under US pressure
2021 suspension of F-35 sale discussions over Chinese ties

military.summary: The US maintains approximately 3,500 military personnel at Al Dhafra Air Base in Abu Dhabi, a key facility for Gulf operations. CENTCOM coordinates closely with UAE armed forces. UAE is one of the largest buyers of US defense equipment (F-16 fighters, THAAD, Patriot missiles). The F-35 sale (~$23B package) remains in limbo pending UAE Huawei resolution. US-UAE conduct regular joint exercises.
incidents: F-35 sale suspended 2021 pending Chinese technology concerns

financial.summary: The US dollar peg anchors UAE monetary policy to the Federal Reserve. US banks (JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs, Citi) dominate UAE investment banking. ADIA has massive US equity and real estate holdings. US private equity firms (KKR, Blackstone, Apollo) are deeply embedded in UAE sovereign fund investment programs. US-UAE financial integration is extremely deep.

energy.summary: The US is now itself a major oil and LNG exporter, reducing its energy dependence on Gulf imports. However, US companies (ExxonMobil, Occidental) retain equity stakes in UAE upstream production. Chevron was awarded an ADNOC upstream concession. US-UAE energy relations have evolved from dependency to partnership.

scientific.summary: Extensive scientific and educational collaboration. NYU Abu Dhabi is a flagship joint institution. MIT, Cornell (Weill Medical College in Qatar shared programs), and other US universities have UAE partnerships. UAE's AI ambitions (TII, Mohamed Bin Zayed University of AI) rely heavily on US-trained researchers. Space cooperation through NASA-UAE collaborations.
